---
title: "Services"
body-classes: "ed-page"
format:
  html:
    page-layout: full
    code-tools: false
---

```{=html}
<main class="ed">

<section class="l-hero">
  <div class="l-wrap">
    <p class="l-eyebrow">Services</p>
    <h1>Decision-grade statistics and analytics for teams that need to act.</h1>
    <p class="l-lede">
      Epsilon Labs delivers statistical consulting, predictive modeling, and
      interactive analytics on a project or retained basis. Every engagement
      is scoped, documented, and built to be defended in front of the people
      who depend on it.
    </p>
    <div class="l-cta-row">
      <a href="#contact" class="l-btn">Start a conversation</a>
      <a href="#practice-areas" class="l-btn l-btn--ghost">See practice areas</a>
    </div>
  </div>
</section>

<section class="l-section first" id="practice-areas">
  <div class="l-wrap">
    <p class="l-eyebrow">Practice areas</p>
    <h2>What we do</h2>
    <div class="l-cols">
      <div>
        <h3>Statistical analysis &amp; modeling</h3>
        <p>Experimental design, hypothesis testing, regression, Bayesian inference, survival analysis. We choose the right method for the question and communicate the answer in plain language.</p>
      </div>
      <div>
        <h3>Predictive modeling &amp; ML</h3>
        <p>Classification, forecasting, and risk scoring with proper validation. Models built to hold up under audit, not just on the holdout set.</p>
      </div>
      <div>
        <h3>Interactive analytics &amp; dashboards</h3>
        <p>R Shiny, Quarto, and lightweight web tools that turn an analysis into a self-service decision aid for stakeholders.</p>
      </div>
      <div>
        <h3>Reproducible reporting</h3>
        <p>R, Python, and SQL pipelines and Quarto-based reports that document assumptions, version cleanly, and rerun reliably from quarter to quarter.</p>
      </div>
      <div>
        <h3>Statistical training &amp; curriculum</h3>
        <p>Workshops and applied curricula for analytics teams, faculties, and students. We help people move from "I ran the test" to "I can defend the choice."</p>
      </div>
      <div>
        <h3>Independent methodology review</h3>
        <p>Outside review of in-house statistical work ahead of a leadership decision, audit, or external release. Tight feedback, written up, no hand-holding.</p>
      </div>
    </div>
  </div>
</section>

<section class="l-section">
  <div class="l-wrap">
    <p class="l-eyebrow">Engagement model</p>
    <h2>How we work together</h2>
    <ul class="l-list">
      <li>
        <div class="l-list-label">1. Scoping call</div>
        <div class="l-list-body">A 30-minute conversation to understand the question, the data, the timeline, and the decision the analysis needs to support. No charge, no obligation.</div>
      </li>
      <li>
        <div class="l-list-label">2. Written proposal</div>
        <div class="l-list-body">A short document covering the approach, deliverables, milestones, fee, and what is explicitly out of scope. You sign it or pass on it. No retainer required to receive one.</div>
      </li>
      <li>
        <div class="l-list-label">3. Project or retainer</div>
        <div class="l-list-body">Fixed-fee projects for bounded analytical work. Monthly retainer for ongoing support across a quarter or year. Either way, every billable hour ties back to a deliverable.</div>
      </li>
      <li>
        <div class="l-list-label">4. Handoff</div>
        <div class="l-list-body">Source code, documentation, and a written debrief. Your team owns the result and can re-run it without us.</div>
      </li>
    </ul>
  </div>
</section>

<section class="l-section">
  <div class="l-wrap">
    <p class="l-eyebrow">Principles</p>
    <h2>What you can expect</h2>
    <div class="l-cols">
      <div>
        <h3>Honest scoping</h3>
        <p>If a question can't be answered with the data you have, we say so before you sign. Referral if we are not the right fit.</p>
      </div>
      <div>
        <h3>Documented assumptions</h3>
        <p>Every analysis ships with the assumptions written down — sample, exclusions, transformations, and the limits of the conclusion.</p>
      </div>
      <div>
        <h3>Reproducible by default</h3>
        <p>Code and data pipelines are versioned. A rerun next quarter produces the same numbers, or tells you why it can't.</p>
      </div>
      <div>
        <h3>Readable for decision-makers</h3>
        <p>Reports lead with the answer, not the method. Stakeholders can act on the first paragraph; the methodology defends it.</p>
      </div>
    </div>
  </div>
</section>

<section class="l-section">
  <div class="l-wrap">
    <div class="l-contact" id="contact">
      <p class="l-eyebrow">Engagements</p>
      <h2>Have a question worth answering carefully?</h2>
      <p>Tell us the question, the data you have, and when you need the answer. We will reply within two business days with next steps or a referral.</p>
      <div class="l-cta-row" style="justify-content:center;">
        <a class="l-btn" href="mailto:briancervantesalvarez@gmail.com">Email Epsilon Labs</a>
        <a class="l-btn l-btn--ghost" href="/content/privacy/#contact">Use the contact form</a>
      </div>
    </div>
  </div>
</section>

</main>
```
